repro_steps: 2. Observe that provider.buildRemoveLiquidityTransactions is called with shouldClaimAndClose: false
3. Note that provider.buildClaimFeesTransactions is NEVER called
4. Fees accrued in the position are silently forfeited

failing_tests.name: ISSUE #12: SolanaExecutor.close should claim fees before removing liquidity
failing_tests.actual: buildClaimFeesTransactions is NOT called - fees are skipped

failing_tests.name: ISSUE #12: Edge case - no fees accumulated should not cause error
failing_tests.expected: Claim fee operation succeeds even with zero fees
failing_tests.actual: Cannot verify since claimFees is never called

edge_cases.expected_behavior: claimFees should succeed (no-op on Meteora if no fees)
All accrued fees should be claimed before liquidity removal
Should throw error (must use task intent architecture)
Should throw clear error 'not found on-chain'

caveats: Tests mock the provider methods to track call order rather than testing real Meteora SDK behavior
Actual execution will fail with mock RPC pool - this is expected, we only verify call order
Test must be compiled before running: npx tsc in apps/engine directory
out_of_scope: Integration test with real Meteora SDK on devnet
Performance testing of sequential vs atomic transaction approaches
Adding retry logic for failed fee claims
